wheat.label: Wheat
wheat.description: Global average water footprint for wheat production

rice.label: Rice
rice.description: Paddy rice — highly water-intensive due to flooded fields

sugarcane.label: Sugarcane
sugarcane.description: Relatively low per kg but grown in huge volumes

cotton.label: Cotton
cotton.description: One of the most water-intensive crops globally

soybean.label: Soybean
soybean.description: Mostly rain-fed; heavily grown in South America

potato.label: Potato
potato.description: One of the most water-efficient staple crops

tomato.label: Tomato
tomato.description: Moderate water use — common in drip-irrigated systems

apple.label: Apple
apple.description: Fruit orchards require consistent water year-round

banana.label: Banana
banana.description: Tropical fruit grown in high-rainfall regions

mango.label: Mango
mango.description: Popular tropical drupe — water-intensive in dry climates

groundnut.label: Groundnut (Peanut)
groundnut.description: Largely rain-fed legume, significant grey water

sunflower.label: Sunflower
sunflower.description: Oil-seed crop with high water demand

tea.label: Tea
tea.description: High rainfall regions reduce blue water but totals remain high

tropical.label: Tropical
tropical.description: High rainfall reduces blue water dependency

arid.label: Arid / Desert
arid.description: Low rainfall increases irrigation demand significantly

semi_arid.label: Semi-Arid
semi_arid.description: Moderate water stress — supplemental irrigation common

continental.label: Continental
continental.description: Cold winters, warm summers — seasonal water stress

mediterranean.label: Mediterranean
mediterranean.description: Dry summers drive heavy irrigation for summer crops

humid_subtropical.label: Humid Subtropical
humid_subtropical.description: High humidity reduces crop water stress

drip.label: Drip Irrigation
drip.description: Highest efficiency — water delivered directly to roots

surface.label: Surface / Flood
surface.description: Traditional method — high evaporation and runoff losses

furrow.label: Furrow Irrigation
furrow.description: Channels water between crop rows — moderate losses

rainfed.label: Rain-fed (No Irrigation)
rainfed.description: Depends entirely on natural rainfall — no blue water added

subsurface_drip.label: Subsurface Drip
subsurface_drip.description: Most efficient — minimal evaporation, no surface wetting

loamy.label: Loamy
loamy.description: Ideal soil — good water retention and drainage

sandy.label: Sandy
sandy.description: Poor water retention — more irrigation needed

clay.label: Clay
clay.description: High retention but poor drainage — waterlogging risk

silt.label: Silty
silt.description: Good moisture retention — slightly better than loam

peaty.label: Peaty
peaty.description: Excellent moisture retention — common in wetland farms

rabi.label: Rabi (Winter)
rabi.description: Cool and dry — moderate irrigation needed

zaid.label: Zaid (Summer)
zaid.description: Hot dry season — highest irrigation demand

winter.label: Winter
winter.description: Cold temperatures — some crops need frost protection

references: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2011) — The green, blue and grey water footprint of crops and derived crop products
FAO AQUASTAT — Global Information System on Water and Agriculture
Hoekstra et al. (2011) — The Water Footprint Assessment Manual
